- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init
- review
- security-review

content.thinking: The user wants me to read several files from the `ans-core` crate and one from `ans-proto` crate. Let me start by reading all of them in parallel.

content.content:
1	use std::time::Duration;
2	
3	use async_trait::async_trait;
4	
5	use crate::distill::{DistillMode, DistilledPage};
6	use crate::session::ActionOutcome;
7	
8	/// The abstract browser interface.
9	///
10	/// Every browser engine (Chromium CDP, Servo, Firefox, WebKit) implements
11	/// this trait. The rest of the system never touches a concrete backend —
12	/// it only calls methods on `dyn BrowserBackend`.
13	///
14	/// # Safety
15	///
16	/// Implementations must guarantee that calling [`close`](Self::close) (or
17	/// dropping the backend) terminates the underlying browser process.
18	/// Zombie processes are a hard bug.
19	#[async_trait]
20	pub trait BrowserBackend: Send + Sync {
21	    /// Navigate to a URL. Returns page metadata after load completes.
22	    async fn navigate(&self, url: &str, timeout: Duration) -> Result<PageState, BrowserError>;
23	
24	    /// Get the current page's DOM as a distilled tree.
25	    async fn get_distilled_dom(&self, mode: DistillMode) -> Result<DistilledPage, BrowserError>;
26	
27	    /// Capture a screenshot. Returns raw PNG or JPEG bytes.
28	    async fn capture_screenshot(
29	        &self,
30	        format: ImageFormat,
31	        full_page: bool,
32	    ) -> Result<Vec<u8>, BrowserError>;
33	
34	    /// Click an element by CSS selector.
35	    async fn click(&self, selector: &str) -> Result<ActionOutcome, BrowserError>;
36	
37	    /// Type text into an element identified by CSS selector.
38	    async fn type_text(&self, selector: &str, text: &str) -> Result<ActionOutcome, BrowserError>;
39	
40	    /// Select an option from a `<select>` element.
41	    async fn select_option(
42	        &self,
43	        selector: &str,
44	        value: &str,
45	    ) -> Result<ActionOutcome, BrowserError>;
46	
47	    /// Scroll the page by pixels or to a selector.
48	    async fn scroll(
49	        &self,
50	        pixels: Option<i32>,
51	        selector: Option<&str>,
52	    ) -> Result<ActionOutcome, BrowserError>;
53	
54	    /// Wait for the page to reach a stable loaded state.
55	    async fn wait_for_load(&self, timeout: Duration) -> Result<PageState, BrowserError>;
56	
57	    /// Execute arbitrary JavaScript. Returns JSON-serialized result.
58	    /// Only used for fallback operations; prefer typed CDP commands.
59	    async fn execute_script(&self, script: &str) -> Result<String, BrowserError>;
60	
61	    /// Close the browser and clean up all resources.
62	    async fn close(&self) -> Result<(), BrowserError>;
63	}
64	
65	/// Cheap snapshot of what's currently visible in the viewport.
66	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
67	pub struct PageState {
68	    pub url: String,
69	    pub title: String,
70	    pub loaded: bool,
71	    pub dom_node_count: u64,
72	    pub visible_text: Vec<String>,
73	    pub timestamp: chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>,
74	}
75	
76	/// Screenshot output format.
77	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
78	pub enum ImageFormat {
79	    Png,
80	    Jpeg { quality: u8 },
81	}
82	
83	/// Errors that can bubble up from any browser backend.
84	#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
85	pub enum BrowserError {
86	    #[error("navigation timeout after {0:?}")]
87	    NavigationTimeout(Duration),
88	
89	    #[error("selector not found: {0}")]
90	    SelectorNotFound(String),
91	
92	    #[error("element not interactable: {0}")]
93	    NotInteractable(String),
94	
95	    #[error("CDP protocol error: {0}")]
96	    CdpProtocol(String),
97	
98	    #[error("browser process died unexpectedly")]
99	    BrowserCrashed,
100	
101	    #[error("browser closed")]
102	    BrowserClosed,
103	
104	    #[error("connection lost: {0}")]
105	    ConnectionLost(String),
106	
107	    #[error("internal error: {0}")]
108	    Internal(#[from] anyhow::Error),
109	}
110

1	use std::collections::HashMap;
2	
3	/// What the agent is currently doing (or not doing).
4	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
5	pub enum SessionStatus {
6	    Idle,
7	    Navigating,
8	    Executing,
9	    Perceiving,
10	    Blocked,
11	    Failed,
12	}
13	
14	/// An action the agent wants to execute.
15	#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
16	pub struct Action {
17	    pub action_type: ActionType,
18	    pub selector: Option<String>,
19	    pub value: Option<String>,
20	    pub params: HashMap<String, String>,
21	}
22	
23	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
24	pub enum ActionType {
25	    Click,
26	    Type,
27	    Scroll,
28	    Select,
29	    Navigate,
30	    Wait,
31	    Submit,
32	    Screenshot,
33	}
34	
35	impl ActionType {
36	    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
37	        match self {
38	            Self::Click => "click",
39	            Self::Type => "type",
40	            Self::Scroll => "scroll",
41	            Self::Select => "select",
42	            Self::Navigate => "navigate",
43	            Self::Wait => "wait",
44	            Self::Submit => "submit",
45	            Self::Screenshot => "screenshot",
46	        }
47	    }
48	}
49	
50	/// The immediate result of executing an action.
51	#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
52	pub struct ActionOutcome {
53	    pub success: bool,
54	    pub error_message: Option<String>,
55	    pub execution_time_ms: u64,
56	    pub page_url_after: Option<String>,
57	    pub dom_changed: bool,
58	}
59

1	[package]
2	name = "ans-core"
3	version.workspace = true
4	edition.workspace = true
5	description = "Core domain types, traits, and enums shared across Agent Nervous System crates"
6	
7	[lib]
8	path = "src/lib.rs"
9	
10	[dependencies]
11	ans-proto.workspace = true
12	async-trait.workspace = true
13	uuid.workspace = true
14	chrono.workspace = true
15	serde.workspace = true
16	thiserror.workspace = true
17	anyhow.workspace = true
18	tracing.workspace = true
19

1	//! Compiled protobuf types and gRPC service definitions for the
2	//! Agent Nervous System.
3	//!
4	//! This crate re-exports the generated code from `proto/ans.proto`.
5	//! All other crates depend on this one for the gRPC contract.
6	
7	#![deny(unsafe_code, rust_2018_idioms)]
8	#![allow(missing_docs, unused_imports)]
9	
10	/// Module containing all generated proto types.
11	///
12	/// Generated by `tonic_build` from `proto/ans.proto`.
13	/// The package name in the proto file is `ans`, so tonic
14	/// generates an `ans` module with all message types and
15	/// the gRPC service client/server.
16	pub mod ans {
17	    tonic::include_proto!("ans");
18	}
19

1   use std::time::Duration;
2   
3   use async_trait::async_trait;

5   use crate::distill::{DistillMode, DistilledPage};
6   use crate::session::ActionOutcome;
7   
8   /// The abstract browser interface.
9   ///
10  /// Every browser engine (Chromium CDP, Servo, Firefox, WebKit) implements
11  /// this trait. The rest of the system never touches a concrete backend —
12  /// it only calls methods on `dyn BrowserBackend`.
13  ///
14  /// # Safety
15  ///
16  /// Implementations must guarantee that calling [`close`](Self::close) (or
17  /// dropping the backend) terminates the underlying browser process.
18  /// Zombie processes are a hard bug.
19  #[async_trait]
20  pub trait BrowserBackend: Send + Sync {
21      /// Navigate to a URL. Returns page metadata after load completes.
22      async fn navigate(&self, url: &str, timeout: Duration) -> Result<PageState, BrowserError>;
23  
24      /// Get the current page's DOM as a distilled tree.
25      async fn get_distilled_dom(&self, mode: DistillMode) -> Result<DistilledPage, BrowserError>;
26  
27      /// Capture a screenshot. Returns raw PNG or JPEG bytes.
28      async fn capture_screenshot(
29          &self,
30          format: ImageFormat,
31          full_page: bool,
32      ) -> Result<Vec<u8>, BrowserError>;
33  
34      /// Click an element by CSS selector.
35      async fn click(&self, selector: &str) -> Result<ActionOutcome, BrowserError>;
36  
37      /// Type text into an element identified by CSS selector.
38      async fn type_text(&self, selector: &str, text: &str) -> Result<ActionOutcome, BrowserError>;
39  
40      /// Select an option from a `<select>` element.
41      async fn select_option(
42          &self,
43          selector: &str,
44          value: &str,
45      ) -> Result<ActionOutcome, BrowserError>;
46  
47      /// Scroll the page by pixels or to a selector.
48      async fn scroll(
49          &self,
50          pixels: Option<i32>,
51          selector: Option<&str>,
52      ) -> Result<ActionOutcome, BrowserError>;
53  
54      /// Wait for the page to reach a stable loaded state.
55      async fn wait_for_load(&self, timeout: Duration) -> Result<PageState, BrowserError>;
56  
57      /// Execute arbitrary JavaScript. Returns JSON-serialized result.
58      /// Only used for fallback operations; prefer typed CDP commands.
59      async fn execute_script(&self, script: &str) -> Result<String, BrowserError>;
60  
61      /// Close the browser and clean up all resources.
62      async fn close(&self) -> Result<(), BrowserError>;
63  }
64  
65  /// Cheap snapshot of what's currently visible in the viewport.
66  #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
67  pub struct PageState {
68      pub url: String,
69      pub title: String,
70      pub loaded: bool,
71      pub dom_node_count: u64,

79      Png,
80      Jpeg { quality: u8 },
81  }
82  
83  /// Errors that can bubble up from any browser backend.
84  #[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
85  pub enum BrowserError {
86      #[error("navigation timeout after {0:?}")]
87      NavigationTimeout(Duration),
88

91

1   //! Core domain types for the Agent Nervous System.
2   //!
3   //! This crate defines the shared vocabulary: the [`BrowserBackend`] trait,
4   //! domain enums ([`DistillMode`], [`SessionStatus`], [`GoalStatus`]),
5   //! and the [`DecisionRecord`] that powers the Decision Intelligence Layer.
6   //!
7   //! Wire-format types (gRPC messages) live in `ans-proto`. This crate
8   //! defines the domain layer above them — traits that backends implement,
9   //! enums that drive state machines, and records that the scoring engine
10  //! stores and queries.

## Summary of the architecture

### `BrowserBackend` trait (in `backend.rs`)

A `Send + Sync` async trait (via `async_trait`) that abstracts any browser engine. Its methods are:

| Method | Signature | Returns |
|---|---|---|
| `navigate` | `(&self, url: &str, timeout: Duration)` | `Result<PageState, BrowserError>` |
| `get_distilled_dom` | `(&self, mode: DistillMode)` | `Result<DistilledPage, BrowserError>` |
| `capture_screenshot` | `(&self, format: ImageFormat, full_page: bool)` | `Result<Vec<u8>, BrowserError>` |
| `click` | `(&self, selector: &str)` | `Result<ActionOutcome, BrowserError>` |
| `type_text` | `(&self, selector: &str, text: &str)` | `Result<ActionOutcome, BrowserError>` |
| `select_option` | `(&self, selector: &str, value: &str)` | `Result<ActionOutcome, BrowserError>` |
| `scroll` | `(&self, pixels: Option<i32>, selector: Option<&str>)` | `Result<ActionOutcome, BrowserError>` |
| `wait_for_load` | `(&self, timeout: Duration)` | `Result<PageState, BrowserError>` |
| `execute_script` | `(&self, script: &str)` | `Result<String, BrowserError>` |
| `close` | `(&self)` | `Result<(), BrowserError>` |

Supporting types in the same file:
- **`PageState`** -- url, title, loaded flag, dom_node_count, visible_text, timestamp.
- **`ImageFormat`** -- `Png` or `Jpeg { quality: u8 }`.
- **`BrowserError`** -- an enum with 8 variants via `thiserror::Error`: `NavigationTimeout`, `SelectorNotFound`, `NotInteractable`, `CdpProtocol`, `BrowserCrashed`, `BrowserClosed`, `ConnectionLost`, `Internal(#[from] anyhow::Error)`.

### Session types (in `session.rs`)

- **`SessionStatus`** -- six-variant enum: `Idle`, `Navigating`, `Executing`, `Perceiving`, `Blocked`, `Failed`. Derives `Debug`, `Clone`, `Copy`, `PartialEq`, `Eq`, `Serialize`, `Deserialize`.
- **`Action`** -- has an `action_type: ActionType`, optional `selector` and `value` strings, and a `params: HashMap<String, String>` bag for extra key-value data.
- **`ActionType`** -- eight-variant enum: `Click`, `Type`, `Scroll`, `Select`, `Navigate`, `Wait`, `Submit`, `Screenshot`. Has an `as_str()` method returning the lowercase static string name.
- **`ActionOutcome`** -- the result of executing an action: `success: bool`, `error_message: Option<String>`, `execution_time_ms: u64`, `page_url_after: Option<String>`, `dom_changed: bool`.

### `ans-core` crate structure (`lib.rs` and `Cargo.toml`)

The crate exposes these public modules and re-exports:

| Module | Re-exported types |
|---|---|
| `backend` | `BrowserBackend` (trait), plus the supporting `PageState`, `ImageFormat`, `BrowserError` |
| `budget` | `BudgetMode` |
| `decision` | `BusinessOutcome`, `DecisionRecord` |
| `distill` | `DistillMode`, `DistilledElement`, `InteractiveElement`, `SemanticBlock` |
| `goal` | `GoalContext`, `GoalState`, `GoalStatus`, `SubGoal`, `SubGoalStatus` |
| `immune` | `DistractionKind`, `ImmuneAction`, `InjectionAction`, `InjectionScore` |
| `session` | `Action`, `ActionOutcome`, `SessionStatus` |
| `signal` | `EyeName`, `EyeReportContent`, `RoutedSignal` |

Its sole workspace dependency is `ans-proto` (along with `async-trait`, `uuid`, `chrono`, `serde`, `thiserror`, `anyhow`, `tracing`).

### Proto-generated types (`ans-proto`)

The crate has a single public module `pub mod ans` that calls `tonic::include_proto!("ans")`. This means:
- The proto file lives at `proto/ans.proto` in the workspace root (or the `ans-proto` crate root).
- The proto package name is `ans`.
- `tonic_build` (run at compile time via build script) generates all message types, enums, and gRPC client/server stubs into the `ans` module.
- Consumers import these as `ans_proto::ans::SomeMessage` or `ans_proto::ans::some_service_client::SomeServiceClient`.

A key design note: `ans-core` depends on `ans-proto` but does **not** re-export any of the proto types in its `lib.rs`. The wire-format gRPC types remain behind the `ans_proto::ans::` path. The domain types (`Action`, `SessionStatus`, `BrowserBackend`, etc.) defined in `ans-core` are the ones consumed by the rest of the system, while the proto-generated types serve as the serialization/transport layer.
